notes: Country-level VIZA RAG seed for laos. This file is the source of truth for this country's visitor/tourism visa knowledge and is designed to evolve alongside the country-specific form-filling workflow.

chunks.title: Chinese passport tourism route for Laos
chunks.content: For Chinese ordinary passport holders planning short tourism travel to Laos, VIZA AI should consider the official Laos tourist eVisa route or visa-on-arrival/consular route depending on entry point. First confirm nationality, stay length, entry port, passport type, tourism purpose, and whether the port accepts eVisa. Laos eVisa is for ordinary passports and tourism-related short stays. Keep advice limited to tourism/visitor travel unless the user states work, study, residence, immigration, journalism, volunteering or another regulated purpose.

chunks.title: Laos tourist eVisa scope
chunks.content: The official Laos eVisa portal describes Laos e-Visa as a Tourist eVisa for short stays related to tourism, sightseeing, cultural visits and short family trips. It is for ordinary passports, not diplomatic, official or emergency travel documents. VIZA AI should check accepted entry points and avoid recommending eVisa for non-tourism or ineligible passport types.

chunks.title: Laos document and port direction
chunks.content: For Laos, ask for entry point, travel dates, passport validity, photo/document upload readiness, and whether the traveller will enter by air or land. Tell users to use the official eVisa portal and carry the eVisa approval document with the same passport used for the application.

documents.title: Laos Application Form and Document Intake Requirements

chunks.title: Laos application channel and form scope
chunks.content: Laos official eVisa portal Official source URLs reviewed for this seed: https://www.evisagov.la/official/en-us/evisa. This RAG chunk is for visitor/tourism-oriented intake and form-filling preparation; route work, study, residence, immigration, journalism or other regulated purposes to a separate country workflow.

chunks.title: Laos fields to collect before filling the form
chunks.content: identity, ordinary passport details, photo, travel dates, arrival port, contact details and tourism purpose

chunks.title: Laos supporting documents and review checklist
chunks.content: passport biodata, photo, accepted entry port confirmation and eVisa approval document Workflow guardrails: VIZA should separate eligibility check, form filling, document upload, payment and post-approval travel/entry checks. Do not guarantee entry; border authorities make the final decision.

chunks.title: Standard passport identity-field answer rules
chunks.content: Standard-answer identity fields must be answered from the applicant's passport biodata page, MRZ, official identity document, or the official dropdown options. This applies to passport number, name, date of birth, sex, nationality, issuing country, issue date, expiry date, issuing authority, place of issue, and passport/document type. For issuing authority or place-of-issue questions, do not infer the answer from the pickup city, residence city, application country, or travel destination; copy the exact Authority / Issuing authority / Place of issue wording printed on the passport if present. For Chinese ordinary passports, newer valid passports may show National Immigration Administration, PRC / 中华人民共和国国家移民管理局; older valid passports may show MPS Exit & Entry Administration / 公安部出入境管理局. If the passport prints a different authority, use the passport text. For passport type, select Ordinary / Regular / Normal only for ordinary personal passports; select Diplomatic, Official, Service, Special, Travel document, Refugee, or Other only when the document explicitly indicates that type. Official sources reviewed for this standard field rule include China National Immigration Administration certificate-issuing-authority notices and the Passport Law of the People's Republic of China; the passport biodata page remains the final source for the applicant's individual answer.
